evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG/test/debug requester through tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or simulation-only SimDTM/SimJTAG paths
evidence_slots.operation: DMI debug request/response transactions that can reach the debug module and exported hart debug controls
evidence_slots.object: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug module DMI request path
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG/debug requests that can halt/reset/control harts or access debug module state should be gated by an authorization/unlock condition such as jtag_unlock derived from jtag_hash/lifecycle/test policy.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals instantiates dmi_jtag with jtag_hash_i/okey_hash_i/ikey_hash_i and jtag_unlock_o, and connects dmi_req_o/dmi_req_valid_o directly to debug_req/debug_req_valid. SimDTM is compiled under RISCV_FESVR_SIM and bypasses dmi_jtag. The provided observations do not include the debug module instantiation or dmi_jtag internals, so it is not proven whether DMI operations or debug outputs are gated by jtag_unlock.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 244-335 for SimDTM/dmi_jtag/SimJTAG; debug module signals declared around 337-351
evidence_slots.state_condition: normal JTAG build, RISCV_FESVR_SIM build, RISCV_JTAG_SIM build, reset/unlocked state
evidence_slots.impact: If DMI requests reach the debug module without authentication, an external or simulated debug subject could halt/reset/control harts or access debug module state.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need dmi_jtag internals and debug module instantiation/use sites for debug_req_valid/debug_req/debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o to close whether jtag_unlock is enforced before protected debug effects.
inspection_records.notes: jtag_unlock is visibly produced by dmi_jtag and also passed to reglk_wrapper, but the observed top-level snippets do not show it gating the debug request path.

evidence_slots.subject: debug-mode requester through AXI/peripheral wrapper
evidence_slots.operation: read/write/configure crypto peripheral registers in debug mode
evidence_slots.object: RNG polynomial registers, seed readback, random segments, cs_state; RSA wrapper registers also in scope but not fully closed by provided snippets
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug-mode access to crypto/RNG wrapper registers should be covered by the same or stronger access-control/lock policy as normal software access, unless debug is explicitly authenticated by the top-level debug unlock.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top level passes debug_mode_i directly into rng_wrapper at riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1105-1112 with acct_ctrl_i=acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][10]. In rng_wrapper, en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i, but read cases 15-25 return seed128/64/32/16, rand segments, and cs_state when debug_mode_i is asserted regardless of reglk_ctrl_i[7]. The write-side snippet shows poly128[3] is written as debug_mode_i ? wdata : (reglk_ctrl_i[5] ? old : wdata), bypassing reglk in debug mode. No observed top-level connection ties debug_mode_i to jtag_unlock.
evidence_slots.state_condition: debug_mode_i asserted while reglk_ctrl_i lock bits are set and acct_ctrl_i permits wrapper transaction
evidence_slots.impact: A requester able to assert/use debug_mode_i can modify RNG polynomial configuration or read internal RNG seed/state despite lock bits, enabling predictable randomness or leakage of RNG internal state.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: RSA debug_mode_i use was not fully observed in the provided snippets; the candidate finding is supported by RNG evidence. Need top-level provenance/authorization of debug_mode_i to prove whether it is trusted.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: rng_wrapper uses debug_mode_i to bypass lock-based protection on sensitive RNG configuration/readback; no observed relationship ties debug_mode_i to authenticated jtag_unlock.
inspection_records.notes: acct_ctrl_i still gates en, so the observed bypass is specifically against reglk protection, not a complete acct_ctrl bypass in the shown RNG code.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/NoC requester
evidence_slots.object: PKT fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o and pkey/fuse read controls; RNG polynomial and internal-state readback controls; top-level reglk_ctrl array
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Once a lock bit controlling a protected peripheral/register is set, all software-visible write paths to that protected register and to the lock bit itself should be blocked or sticky until reset, including debug/test paths.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: pkt_wrapper gates bus access with acct_ctrl_i but writes fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o on en&&we without checking reglk_ctrl_i. Its pkey/fuse reads are partially masked by reglk_ctrl_i[4:6]. rng_wrapper masks normal reads/writes with reglk bits but uses debug_mode_i to bypass reglk on seed/segment/cs_state reads and at least one polynomial write. reglk_wrapper receives jtag_unlock and outputs the global reglk_ctrl, but no provided reglk_wrapper internals prove lock stickiness or clear-only-on-reset.
evidence_slots.path: pkt_wrapper.sv lines 68-88 and 91-108; rng_wrapper.sv lines 123-128 and 180-206; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1808-1823
evidence_slots.state_condition: after relevant reglk_ctrl bits are set; attempted PKT writes; attempted RNG debug-mode reads/writes
evidence_slots.impact: Locked security configuration can still be modified or sensitive state selected/read through uncovered or debug-mode paths, undermining the intended lock/sticky protection.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need reglk_wrapper internals to determine whether lock bits themselves are sticky and cannot be cleared except reset. Full wrapper coverage for RSA and other peripherals was not fully observed.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Register-lock coverage is incomplete: PKT fuse request/address writes are not guarded by reglk_ctrl_i, and RNG debug mode bypasses reglk-protected read/write policy.
inspection_records.notes: The provided evidence is sufficient for incomplete use-site coverage even without proving reglk_ctrl storage stickiness.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/NoC requester
evidence_slots.object: PKT, RNG, RSA peripheral register spaces and protected response data
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Access-control bits should mediate all read/write requests to protected peripherals consistently at decode/request acceptance and response data mux sites; denied access should not update state or return protected data.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level derives acc_ctrl_c by privilege index and passes per-peripheral bits into wrappers, e.g. PKT acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5], RNG acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][10], RSA implied from nearby wrapper integration. pkt_wrapper, rng_wrapper, and rsa_wrapper snippets all compute en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i, and their write/read decode logic is nested under en. Boot ROM request is also gated by rom_req_acct&&acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0].
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 214-223, 516-517, 1100-1112, 1623-1633; pkt_wrapper.sv lines 68, 80, 95; rng_wrapper.sv lines 85, 123, 180-211; rsa_wrapper.sv lines 62, 78-80
evidence_slots.state_condition: acct_ctrl_i deny state for read and write transactions
evidence_slots.impact: When acct_ctrl_i is denied, observed wrapper decode logic does not perform protected state updates or return protected register data through the wrapper rdata mux.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Top-level NoC/fabric sources and all wrapper internals were not exhaustively inspected; this record is limited to the provided PKT/RNG/RSA/bootrom evidence.
inspection_records.notes: This does not contradict separate findings where access is allowed but reglk/debug/fuse-specific policy is insufficient.

evidence_slots.subject: PKT peripheral requester / software via AXI
evidence_slots.operation: read/expose fuse data or fuse-derived key metadata
evidence_slots.object: fuse_mem contents including keys/hashes/access-control words; fuse_rdata_i; pkey_loc_o/fuse-derived key location
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Reads of fuse memory or fuse-derived key locations should require an authorized subject and address-range/index check; response data should not be exposed through PKT outputs or AXI read data without acct_ctrl/reglk/debug authorization.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: pkt_wrapper writes fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o under en&&we without reglk or range validation. Its read mux initializes rdata=fuse_rdata_i for any enabled access, explicitly returns fuse_rdata_i at address case 4 when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear, and default only zeroes rdata when fuse_addr_o<=110. The pkt submodule is instantiated with req_i tied to 1'b1 and maps fuse_addr_o to pkey_loc_o. fuse_mem contains secure data constants, including JTAG expected hash, HMAC/AES/SHA keys, RNG polynomials, and access-control words.
evidence_slots.path: pkt_wrapper.sv lines 68, 80-88, 91-108, 115-123; pkt.sv lines 18-70; fuse_mem.sv lines 17-123; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1618-1633
evidence_slots.state_condition: acct_ctrl_i allow state; reglk_ctrl_i[6] clear or default read case with fuse_addr_o>110; arbitrary fuse_addr_o writes
evidence_slots.impact: Authorized-for-PKT but otherwise untrusted software can select and read fuse contents or fuse-derived key-location metadata, potentially exposing cryptographic keys, authentication hashes, and access-control configuration values.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need exact fuse_mem top-level instantiation and full rdata behavior after line 127 to confirm timing, but the PKT read-return path and fuse contents are visible.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: PKT can drive arbitrary fuse addresses and expose fuse data through its AXI read path with only acct_ctrl mediation and incomplete lock/range checks.
inspection_records.notes: Even if acct_ctrl_i is required, the obligation expected address/index mediation and stronger protection for fuse secrets; the observed PKT interface provides broad selection/read capability.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG/debug requester or software-visible fuse reader
evidence_slots.operation: expose/use authentication material and set unlock state
evidence_slots.object: JTAG authentication hash and unlock state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG unlock comparison/authentication should use the intended fuse hash without exposing enough information for unauthorized unlock; jtag_hash_o should not be readable by unauthenticated software/debug paths.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: fuse_mem declares jtag_hash_o/ikey_hash_o/okey_hash_o and stores JTAG expected HMAC hash words at the start of its mem constant. dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash, ikey_hash, and okey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock. Separately, PKT can write fuse_addr_o and read fuse_rdata_i; fuse_mem mem includes the JTAG hash values and other secrets. No observed PKT range check excludes the JTAG hash area.
evidence_slots.path: fuse_mem.sv lines 8, 15-23; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 275-284; pkt_wrapper.sv lines 80-108
evidence_slots.state_condition: normal operation before/after failed authentication; PKT acct_ctrl_i allow state
evidence_slots.impact: Exposure of expected JTAG hash/auth material can aid unauthorized debug unlock attempts and undermines the confidentiality of debug authentication assets.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full top-level fuse_mem instantiation wiring to jtag_hash/ikey_hash/okey_hash and exact address indexing order to identify all readable hash words. Hash may be intended public by architecture, but the provided comments describe fuse_mem as secure data.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: JTAG authentication hash material is present in fuse_mem and is also reachable through the PKT/fuse read mechanism under the observed broad fuse read path.
inspection_records.notes: The finding relies on the combination of secure fuse constants and broad PKT fuse read access.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible RNG peripheral requester
evidence_slots.operation: write seed/poly/load and read RNG/internal state
evidence_slots.object: poly128/poly64/poly32/poly16 configuration, rand_num, rand_num_valid, seed128/64/32/16 outputs, rand segments, cs_state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Configuration of RNG seed, polynomial, load, and any readback of internal seeds/segments should be mediated by access-control and lock bits; unprivileged or denied requesters should not force predictable RNG state or observe internal seed state.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rng_wrapper gates wrapper decode with en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i. It resets/load-generates deterministic seed values 2,3,3,2 when load & ~debug_mode_i. Write-side evidence shows polynomial writes can use debug_mode_i to ignore reglk_ctrl_i[5]. Read cases 15-25 return seed outputs, rand segments, and cs_state when debug_mode_i is asserted regardless of reglk_ctrl_i[7]; rand_num and rand_num_valid are also MMIO-readable under lock-dependent conditions. Top-level bridges response data to ariane_rng_buf_noc3_data_o.
evidence_slots.state_condition: debug_mode_i asserted; after lock bit set; acct_ctrl_i allow state
evidence_slots.impact: A requester can make RNG behavior predictable by changing RNG polynomials or observe internal RNG state/segments despite lock bits, weakening consumers that rely on unpredictable random output.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need full write map lines 128-179 to enumerate every writable RNG register and confirm all seed/load write behavior.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: RNG wrapper exposes configuration and internal state readback; debug_mode_i bypasses lock masking for seeds, random segments, and control-state readback, and can bypass lock on polynomial writes.
inspection_records.notes: The evidence strongly supports sensitive readback and debug lock bypass for multiple internal RNG signals.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible RSA peripheral requester
evidence_slots.object: RSA prime/modulus inputs, msg_in, encrypt/decrypt control, msg_out, operation status and wrapper registers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: RSA key/modulus/message/control registers and result readout should require authorized access and obey lock bits; debug or reset-specific paths should not clear locks or expose stale/plaintext/intermediate data.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rsa_wrapper gates decode with en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i. Write-side snippets show control, prime_i, prime1_i, and msg_in fields are written only when en&&we and use reglk_ctrl_i[3] to retain old values when locked. Reset clears inter_rst_ni, inter_rst1_ni, encry_decry_i, prime_i, prime1_i, and msg_in. The provided observations do not include the read mux, complete debug_mode_i use, msg_out readout policy, or operation-completion clearing behavior.
evidence_slots.path: rsa_wrapper.sv lines 46-63, 66-88, 180-244; rsa_top.v ports from fact layer
evidence_slots.state_condition: access denied state; lock set state; debug_mode_i asserted; after operation finish; rst_13 assertion
evidence_slots.impact: If result or internal RSA registers are readable without proper lock/access/debug authorization or not cleared, cryptographic operands/results could be exposed; current evidence does not prove such behavior.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible reset requester
evidence_slots.object: peripheral reset lines rst_5/rst_10/rst_13 and protected wrapper state/locks/access controls
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Software-controllable resets of security peripherals should require authorization and should not be usable to clear locks/access controls or leave protected outputs in unsafe residue states.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level instantiates rst_wrapper connected to a NoC AXI-lite bridge and passes reglk_ctrl[12] plus acct_ctrl_i=acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12] and debug_mode_i. rst_wrapper outputs rst_1..rst_13, including rst_5 to PKT, rst_10 to RNG, and rst_13 to RSA. PKT/RNG/RSA wrappers use local reset conditions such as ~(rst_ni && ~rst_5/10/13). The internals of rst_wrapper and post-reset values of reglk/acct controls were not provided.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 901-942 and 944-983; pkt_wrapper.sv lines 75-79; rng_wrapper.sv lines 87-101, 217-220; rsa_wrapper.sv lines 63, 66-77
evidence_slots.state_condition: lock set then peripheral reset; acct_ctrl deny state; debug/test reset path
evidence_slots.impact: If a software reset can clear protected state or defeat locks/access controls, software could bypass lock protections or leave cryptographic/debug state in unsafe values; current evidence does not prove the reset control policy.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA engine / DMA NoC master
evidence_slots.operation: DMA read/write to peripheral MMIO/fabric endpoints
evidence_slots.object: protected peripheral address spaces: debug, fuse/PKT, RNG, RSA, reglk/acct/reset, crypto peripherals
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMA-originated transactions should preserve requester identity/security attributes and be blocked from protected MMIO/fuse/crypto/debug regions unless explicitly authorized by access-control/range checks.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level exposes a DMA NoC port and instantiates dma_wrapper with reglk_ctrl[8], acct_ctrl_i=acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8], pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, and we_flag_3. It is connected to a NoC AXI-lite bridge. The provided observations do not include dma_wrapper internals or any fabric routing showing whether DMA can target other protected peripheral regions.
evidence_slots.state_condition: DMA targets protected RNG/RSA/PKT/fuse/debug/reglk/acct/reset regions; acct_ctrl deny state; overlapping ranges
evidence_slots.impact: If DMA transactions can reach protected MMIO/fuse/crypto/debug regions without requester-aware range checks, DMA could bypass CPU privilege and wrapper access-control policy.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI requester
evidence_slots.object: CLINT timer interrupt and IPI control registers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes to timer compare/software interrupt registers should be restricted to the intended privileged requester or protected address space; read/write enables from AXI should not be generated solely from valid handshakes without privilege/range mediation where required.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Fact layer identifies priv_lvl_i at top level and axi_lite_interface producing address_o/en_o/we_o for CLINT. Provided observations do not include CLINT integration or axi_lite_interface logic beyond port facts, so privilege/range enforcement for CLINT cannot be determined.
evidence_slots.path: axi_lite_interface.sv ports lines 21-31 from fact layer; riscv_peripherals.sv CLINT NoC ports lines 96-101 from fact layer
evidence_slots.state_condition: unprivileged requester; DMA/NoC requester path; write vs read behavior
evidence_slots.impact: If unprivileged or DMA-originated writes can update CLINT timer/IPI registers, software could spoof interrupts or disrupt timer behavior.

evidence_slots.subject: boot strap/software-readable requester
evidence_slots.object: boot ROM selection, bootrom_bm/linux contents and boot response data
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Boot ROM selection and reads of boot code/configuration should be mediated by intended lifecycle/boot policy; software should not be able to change boot source or read restricted boot ROM contents through unintended routes after boot if policy forbids it.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level instantiates bootrom and bootrom_linux and gates rom_req with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]. However rom_rdata is assigned as (ariane_boot_sel_i) ? rom_rdata_linux : rom_rdata_linux, so ariane_boot_sel_i never selects rom_rdata_bm despite the comment that it selects BM or linux bootrom. Boot ROM read path is exposed through a NoC AXI-lite bridge and acc_ctrl gating.
evidence_slots.state_condition: after reset vs runtime value of ariane_boot_sel_i; NoC read access to ROM
evidence_slots.impact: The system always returns linux boot ROM contents regardless of the boot selection input, which can violate boot policy if baremetal versus linux ROM selection is security- or lifecycle-relevant.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Boot ROM selection signal ariane_boot_sel_i is ineffective: both mux arms select linux boot ROM data, so the intended baremetal/linux boot policy cannot be enforced by the selection input.

4: 
5: module pkt_wrapper #(
6:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
9:     parameter FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 34
10: )(
11:            clk_i,
12:            rst_ni,
13:            reglk_ctrl_i,
14:            acct_ctrl_i,
15:            fuse_req_o,
16:            fuse_addr_o,
17:            fuse_rdata_i,
18:            axi_req_i, 
19:            axi_resp_o,
20:            rst_5
21:        );
22: 
23: 
24:     input  logic                   clk_i;
25:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
26:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock values
27:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
28:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
29:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
30:     output logic                   fuse_req_o;
31:     output logic [31:0]            fuse_addr_o;
32:     input  logic [31:0]            fuse_rdata_i;
33: 
34:     input logic rst_5;
35: 
36: // internal signals
37: 
38: wire [63:0] pkey_loc; 
39: 
40: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
41: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
42: logic                      en, en_acct;
43: logic                      we;
44: logic [63:0] wdata;
45: logic [63:0] rdata;
46: 
47: 
48: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
49:     // -----------------------------
50:     // AXI Interface Logic
51:     // -----------------------------
52:     axi_lite_interface #(
53:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
54:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
55:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
56:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
57:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
58:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
59:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
60:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
61:         .address_o  ( address    ),
62:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
63:         .we_o       ( we         ),
64:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
65:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
66:     );
67: 
68:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
69: 
70: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
71: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
72: // Write side
73: always @(posedge clk_i)
74:     begin
75:         if(~(rst_ni && ~rst_5))
76:             begin
77:                 fuse_req_o <= 0;
78:                 fuse_addr_o <= 0;
79:             end
80:         else if(en && we)
81:             case(address[7:3])
82:                 0:
83:                     fuse_req_o  <= wdata[0];
84:                 1:
85:                     fuse_addr_o <= wdata;
86:                 default:
87:                     ;
88:             endcase
89:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
90: 
91: //// Read side
92: always @(*)
93:     begin
94:       rdata = 64'b0; 
95:       if (en) begin
96:         rdata = fuse_rdata_i; 
97:         case(address[7:3])
98:             0:
99:                 rdata = {31'b0, fuse_req_o};
100:             1:
101:                 rdata = fuse_addr_o;
102:             2:
103:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[63:32];
104:             3:
105:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[5] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[31:0];
106:             4:
107:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[6] ? 'b0 : fuse_rdata_i;
108:             default:
109:                 if (fuse_addr_o <= 110)
110:                     rdata = 32'b0;
111:         endcase
112:       end // if
113:     end // always @ (*)
114: 
115: pkt # (
116:         .FUSE_MEM_SIZE(FUSE_MEM_SIZE)
117:     ) i_pkt(
118:             .clk_i(clk_i),
119:             .rst_ni(rst_ni && ~rst_5),
120:             .req_i(1'b1),
121:             .fuse_indx_i(fuse_addr_o),
122:             .pkey_loc_o(pkey_loc)
123:         );
124: 
125: endmodule

16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
